title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
text: simulated dataset 1200 respondents fictional social-health survey, designed illustrate main features spicy package: variable labels, ordered factors, survey weights, association measures, APA-style reporting.

title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
code: sochealth

title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
text: tibble 1200 rows 24 variables: sex Factor. Sex respondent. age Numeric. Age years (25–75). age_group Ordered factor. Age group (25–34, 35–49, 50–64, 65–75). education Ordered factor. Highest education level (Lower secondary, Upper secondary, Tertiary). social_class Ordered factor. Subjective social class (Lower, Working, Lower middle, Middle, Upper middle). region Factor. Region residence (6 regions). employment_status Factor. Employment status (Employed, Student, Unemployed, Inactive). income_group Ordered factor. Household income group (Low, Lower middle, Upper middle, High). Contains missing values. income Numeric. Monthly household income CHF (1000–7400). smoking Factor. Current smoker (, Yes). Contains missing values. physical_activity Factor. Regular physical activity (, Yes). dentist_12m Factor. Dentist visit last 12 months (, Yes). self_rated_health Ordered factor. Self-rated health (Poor, Fair, Good, good). Contains missing values. wellbeing_score Numeric. -5 wellbeing index (0–100). bmi Numeric. Body mass index kg/m\(^2\) (16–39). Contains missing values. bmi_category Ordered factor. BMI category (Normal weight, Overweight, Obesity). Contains missing values. institutional_trust Ordered factor. Trust institutions (low, Low, High, high). political_position Numeric. Political position 0 (left) 10 (right) scale. Contains missing values. life_sat_health Integer. Satisfaction health (1–5 Likert scale). Contains missing values. life_sat_work Integer. Satisfaction work main activity (1–5 Likert scale). Contains missing values. life_sat_relationships Integer. Satisfaction personal relationships (1–5 Likert scale). Contains missing values. life_sat_standard Integer. Satisfaction standard living (1–5 Likert scale). Contains missing values. response_date POSIXct. Date time survey response (September–November 2024). weight Numeric. Survey design weight (range 0.29–3.45); calibrated sum(weight) matches unweighted N mean(weight) approximately 1. See Details.

title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
text: Simulated data illustration purposes; reproducible sourcing data-raw/sochealth.R. script seeds main generation block set.seed(2025), two missing-value injection blocks set.seed(2027) (four life_sat_* items) set.seed(2026) (smoking, self_rated_health, income_group, political_position, bmi).

title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
text: Every variable carries "label" attribute (read labelled::var_label() surfaced varlist() / code_book()). mix factor types deliberate: nominal factors (sex, region, ...) ordered factors (education, self_rated_health, ...) live side side cross_tab() table_categorical() can demonstrate automatic ordinal-vs-nominal dispatch (Cramer's V, Phi, Kendall's Tau-b, Goodman-Kruskal Gamma) dataset. Survey weights (weight) calibrated: sum(weight) matches unweighted N within rounding (\(\approx 1200\)) mean(weight) \(\approx 1\). Weighted means therefore agree unweighted means sampling noise without rescaling.

title: Simulated social-health survey — sochealth
code: data(sochealth) varlist(sochealth) #> Non-interactive session: use `tbl = TRUE` to return a tibble. freq(sochealth, education) #> Frequency table: education #>  #>  Category   │ Values               Freq.    Percent  #> ────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────── #>  Valid      │ Lower secondary        261       21.8  #>             │ Upper secondary        539       44.9  #>             │ Tertiary               400       33.3  #> ────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────── #>  Total      │                       1200      100.0  #>  #> Label: Highest education level #> Class: ordered, factor #> Data: sochealth cross_tab(sochealth, education, self_rated_health) #> Crosstable: education x self_rated_health (N) #>  #>  Values            │   Poor    Fair    Good    Very good │   Total  #> ───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼───────── #>  Lower secondary   │     28      86     102           44 │     260  #>  Upper secondary   │     28     118     263          118 │     527  #>  Tertiary          │      5      62     193          133 │     393  #> ───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┼───────── #>  Total             │     61     266     558          295 │    1180  #>  #> Chi-2(6) = 73.2, p <.001 #> Kendall's Tau-b = 0.20

title: Somers' D — somers_d
text: somers_d() computes Somers' D two-way contingency table ordinal variables.

title: Somers' D — somers_d
code: somers_d(   x,   direction = c("row", "column", "symmetric"),   detail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95,   digits = 3L,   .include_se = FALSE )

title: Somers' D — somers_d
text: x contingency table (class table). direction Direction prediction: "row" (default, column predicts row), "column" (row predicts column), "symmetric" (average directions). detail Logical. FALSE (default), return estimate numeric scalar. TRUE, return named numeric vector including confidence interval p-value. conf_level number 0 1 giving confidence level (default 0.95). used detail = TRUE. Set NULL omit confidence interval. digits Number decimal places used printing result (default 3). affects detail = TRUE output. .include_se Internal parameter; use.

title: Somers' D — somers_d
text: structure cramer_v(): scalar detail = FALSE, named vector detail = TRUE. p-value tests H0: D = 0 (Wald z-test).

title: Somers' D — somers_d
text: Somers' D asymmetric ordinal measure defined \(d = (C - D) / (C + D + T)\), \(T\) number pairs tied independent variable. symmetric version (direction = "symmetric") harmonic mean two asymmetric values, matching SPSS / PSPP convention; identical Kendall's Tau-b (geometric mean two quantities), although two often agree two decimals. analytic SE / CI reported symmetric form (DescTools follows convention). Standard error formulas asymmetric directions follow DescTools implementations (Signorell et al., 2024); see cramer_v() full references.

title: Somers' D — somers_d
code: tab <- table(sochealth$education, sochealth$self_rated_health) somers_d(tab, direction = "row") #> [1] 0.2015369 somers_d(tab, direction = "column", detail = TRUE) #> Estimate  CI lower  CI upper      p #>    0.208     0.157     0.258  <.001

what: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package
title: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package
text: spicy provides tools descriptive data analysis, variable inspection, tabulation workflows: frequency tables, cross-tabulations chi-squared tests effect sizes, association measures contingency tables, categorical continuous summary tables, model-based linear-regression tables optional additive covariate adjustment, row-wise descriptive summaries, interactive codebooks, variable-label extraction, clipboard export.

title: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package
text: spicy active pre-1.0 development. Breaking changes made deliberately minor-version bumps always announced NEWS.md. API surface partitioned follows; users planning embed spicy production pipelines downstream packages rely stable surface. Stable (signature behaviour preserved across 0.y.z 1.0.0; documented changes ): Frequency / cross-tabs: freq(), cross_tab() Variable inspection: varlist() / vl(), code_book(), label_from_names() Row-wise summaries: mean_n(), sum_n(), count_n() Clipboard export: copy_clipboard() Association measures (point estimates documented CIs): cramer_v(), phi(), contingency_coef(), yule_q(), gamma_gk(), kendall_tau_b(), kendall_tau_c(), somers_d(), lambda_gk(), goodman_kruskal_tau(), uncertainty_coef() Stabilising (still maturing; argument names may tightened 1.0 NEWS.md entry, silent behavioural changes): Summary table builders: table_categorical(), table_continuous(), table_continuous_lm() Omnibus association overview: assoc_measures() Internal API (part public surface; can change without notice – avoid calling directly downstream code): ASCII rendering primitives: build_ascii_table(), spicy_print_table()

title: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package
text: broom::tidy() broom::glance() methods spicy_freq_table, spicy_categorical_table, spicy_continuous_table, spicy_continuous_lm_table follow standard broom column conventions (outcome, term, estimate, std.error, conf.low, conf.high, statistic, p.value, df, df.residual, r.squared, adj.r.squared, nobs, ...). set columns produced method considered stabilising: existing columns silently renamed semantics changed within 0.y.z, breaking change announced NEWS.md. Adding optional new columns (e.g. covariate-adjustment metadata) breaking change. Numeric columns always returned type downstream broom-consumers expect: df integer, df.residual numeric (Satterthwaite-corrected degrees freedom cluster-robust variance modes preserved verbatim, matching lmerTest::glance() afex output convention).

title: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package
text: errors warnings emitted stable / stabilising surfaces carry classed conditions downstream code can dispatch class via tryCatch() / withCallingHandlers() instead matching message strings. condition package-wide parent class plus leaf class describing specific cause: spicy_error Catch-parent every error raised spicy. Leaves: spicy_invalid_input – bad argument value type. spicy_invalid_data – bad data shape (data.frame, NA cells forbidden, length mismatch). spicy_missing_pkg – Suggests dependency required requested operation installed. spicy_missing_column – referenced column data. spicy_unsupported – operation applicable input (e.g., Phi requested non-2x2 table). spicy_warning Catch-parent every warning. Leaves: spicy_undefined_stat – requested statistic undefined input; result NA (e.g., Tau-b table -zero marginals). spicy_dropped_na – NA observations silently excluded computation (e.g., NA weights). spicy_ignored_arg – argument ignored due context (e.g., correct = TRUE non-2x2 table). spicy_no_selection – column selector produced empty set; empty result returned rather erroring. spicy_fallback – requested computation failed; simpler estimator used instead. spicy_summary_failed – varlist() summarise one column; rest table fine. spicy_renamed_column – user data column factor level collided spicy-internal name auto-renamed preserve data (emitted cross_tab()).

title: spicy: descriptive statistics, summary tables, and data management — spicy-package

what: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
text: User-facing helper prints spicy-styled ASCII table console optional title note, table-type-aware alignment defaults, automatic horizontal panelling table wider console. Wraps internal renderer build_ascii_table().

title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
code: spicy_print_table(   x,   title = attr(x, "title"),   note = attr(x, "note"),   padding = 2L,   first_column_line = TRUE,   row_total_line = TRUE,   column_total_line = TRUE,   bottom_line = FALSE,   lines_color = "darkgrey",   align_left_cols = NULL,   align_center_cols = integer(0),   center_headers = FALSE,   spanners = NULL,   group_sep_rows = integer(0),   total_row_idx = attr(x, "total_row_idx"),   display_labels = NULL,   ... )

title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
text: x spicy_table data.frame printed. title Optional title displayed table. Defaults "title" attribute x present. note Optional note displayed table. Defaults "note" attribute x present. padding Non-negative integer giving number extra characters added column's auto-computed width (max cell-content width header width). Defaults 2L. See build_ascii_table() precise formula migration note pre-0.11.0 string enum. first_column_line Logical. TRUE (default), adds vertical separator first column. row_total_line, column_total_line, bottom_line Logical flags controlling presence horizontal lines total rows/columns bottom table. row_total_line column_total_line default TRUE; bottom_line defaults FALSE. lines_color Character. Color table separators. Defaults "darkgrey". applied output supports ANSI colors (see crayon::has_color()). align_left_cols Integer vector column indices left-align. NULL (default), alignment auto-detected based x: freq tables -> c(1, 2) cross tables -> 1 align_center_cols Integer vector column indices center-align. Defaults integer(0). center_headers Logical. TRUE, column headers centered column content even data right-aligned. Passed build_ascii_table(). Defaults FALSE. spanners Optional named list column-group labels (label -> integer column indices). Passed build_ascii_table(); table split horizontal panels panel keeps spanners whose columns fully contained . Defaults NULL (spanner row). group_sep_rows Integer vector row indices light dashed separator line drawn. Defaults integer(0). total_row_idx Optional integer vector 1-based row indices identifying totals rows; defaults "total_row_idx" attribute x (set cross_tab()). See build_ascii_table(). display_labels Optional character vector length ncol(x) used override colnames(x) printed header text . Sliced per panel table split across stacked panels. Forwarded build_ascii_table(); see function full semantics. Defaults NULL. ... Additional arguments passed build_ascii_table().

title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
text: Invisibly returns x, printing formatted ASCII table console.

title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
text: Table type auto-detected x drives default alignment align_left_cols = NULL: frequency table (Category column present): first two columns (Category, Values) left-aligned. cross table (otherwise): first column (row variable) left-aligned. table wider console, split stacked horizontal panels left-identifier columns repeated panel. Unicode line-drawing characters used default; coloured separators drawn terminal supports ANSI colour (crayon::has_color()) fall back monochrome otherwise.

title: Print a spicy-formatted ASCII table — spicy_print_table
code: # Simple demonstration df <- data.frame(   Category = c("Valid", "", "Missing", "Total"),   Values = c("Yes", "No", "NA", ""),   Freq. = c(12, 8, 1, 21),   Percent = c(57.1, 38.1, 4.8, 100.0) )  spicy_print_table(df,   title = "Frequency table: Example",   note = "Class: data.frame\nData: demo" ) #> Frequency table: Example #>  #>  Category   │ Values      Freq.    Percent  #> ────────────┼────────────────────────────── #>  Valid      │ Yes            12       57.1  #>             │ No              8       38.1  #>  Missing    │ NA              1        4.8  #> ────────────┼────────────────────────────── #>  Total      │                21        100  #>  #> Class: data.frame #> Data: demo

title: Spicy table engine — spicy_tables
text: Index page ASCII rendering engine produces every spicy console table (freq(), cross_tab(), table_*() family, association-measure printers). engine supports Unicode line drawing, ANSI colours via crayon (monochrome fallback), automatic colour-aware width detection, configurable integer padding (0L / 2L / 4L), per-column alignment, horizontal panelling tables wider console.

title: Spicy table engine — spicy_tables
text: freq() – one-way frequency tables cross_tab() – two-way cross-tabulations table_categorical(), table_continuous(), table_continuous_lm() – multi-variable summary tables

title: Spicy table engine — spicy_tables
text: spicy_print_table() – user-facing wrapper adds title, note, table-type-aware alignment defaults, panelling. build_ascii_table() – underlying string renderer.

what: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
title: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
text: Computes row-wise sums across selected numeric columns data.frame matrix. Missing values handled per row via min_valid (integer count proportion non-NA values required); rows fail rule return NA. Non-numeric columns dropped silently (set verbose = TRUE see ). Designed flow inside dplyr::mutate(): called without explicit data argument, current data context used.

title: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
code: sum_n(   data = NULL,   select = tidyselect::everything(),   exclude = NULL,   min_valid = NULL,   digits = NULL,   regex = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
text: data data.frame matrix. Optional inside dplyr::mutate(), current grouping/data context used automatically. select Columns include. regex = FALSE, use tidyselect syntax (default: tidyselect::everything()). regex = TRUE, provide regular expression pattern (character string). exclude Columns exclude (default: NULL). min_valid Minimum number valid (non-NA) values required per row. Accepts: NULL (default) – every selected column must valid. proportion (0, 1) – round(ncol(x) * min_valid) valid columns required (e.g. min_valid = 0.5 requires least half selected columns non-NA). non-negative integer count number selected numeric columns. Non-integer values >= 1 (e.g. 1.5) counts greater ncol(x) raise actionable error. digits Optional non-negative integer giving number decimal places round result . Defaults NULL (rounding). regex Logical. FALSE (default), uses tidyselect helpers. TRUE, select argument treated regular expression. verbose Logical. FALSE (default), messages suppressed. TRUE, prints message non-numeric columns excluded.

title: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
text: numeric vector row-wise sums.

title: Row sums with an optional minimum-valid-values rule — sum_n
code: library(dplyr)  # Create a simple numeric data frame df <- tibble(   var1 = c(10, NA, 30, 40, 50),   var2 = c(5, NA, 15, NA, 25),   var3 = c(NA, 30, 20, 50, 10) )  # Compute row-wise sums (all values must be valid by default) sum_n(df) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Require at least 2 valid (non-NA) values per row sum_n(df, min_valid = 2) #> [1] 15 NA 65 90 85  # Require at least 50% valid (non-NA) values per row sum_n(df, min_valid = 0.5) #> [1] 15 NA 65 90 85  # Round the results to 1 decimal sum_n(df, digits = 1) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Select specific columns sum_n(df, select = c(var1, var2)) #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Select specific columns using a pipe df |>   select(var1, var2) |>   sum_n() #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Exclude a column sum_n(df, exclude = "var3") #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Select columns ending with "1" sum_n(df, select = ends_with("1")) #> [1] 10 NA 30 40 50  # Use with native pipe df |> sum_n(select = starts_with("var")) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Use inside dplyr::mutate() df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 2)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        65 #> 4    40    NA    50        90 #> 5    50    25    10        85  # Select columns directly inside mutate() df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(select = c(var1, var2), min_valid = 1)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        45 #> 4    40    NA    50        40 #> 5    50    25    10        75  # Select columns before mutate df |>   select(var1, var2) |>   mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 1)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>    var1  var2 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5        15 #> 2    NA    NA        NA #> 3    30    15        45 #> 4    40    NA        40 #> 5    50    25        75  # Show verbose message df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 2, digits = 1, verbose = TRUE)) #> sum_n(): Row sums computed with min_valid = 2, regex = FALSE #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        65 #> 4    40    NA    50        90 #> 5    50    25    10        85  # Add character and grouping columns df_mixed <- mutate(df,   name = letters[1:5],   group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "A") ) df_mixed #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A     #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A     #> 3    30    15    20 c     B     #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B     #> 5    50    25    10 e     A      # Non-numeric columns are ignored sum_n(df_mixed) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Use inside mutate with mixed data df_mixed |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(select = starts_with("var"))) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A            NA #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A            NA #> 3    30    15    20 c     B            65 #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B            NA #> 5    50    25    10 e     A            85  # Use everything(), but exclude known non-numeric sum_n(df_mixed, select = everything(), exclude = "group") #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Select columns using regex sum_n(df_mixed, select = "^var", regex = TRUE) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85 sum_n(df_mixed, select = "ar", regex = TRUE) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Apply to a subset of rows df_mixed[1:3, ] |> sum_n(select = starts_with("var")) #> [1] NA NA 65  # Store the result in a new column df_mixed$sum_score <- sum_n(df_mixed, select = starts_with("var")) df_mixed #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A            NA #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A            NA #> 3    30    15    20 c     B            65 #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B            NA #> 5    50    25    10 e     A            85  # With a numeric matrix mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, NA, 4, 5, NA, 7, 8, 9), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) mat #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    1    2   NA #> [2,]    4    5   NA #> [3,]    7    8    9 mat |> sum_n(min_valid = 2) #> [1]  3  9 24

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: Builds publication-ready frequency cross-tabulation table one many categorical variables selected tidyselect syntax. , produces grouped cross-tabulation summaries (using cross_tab() internally) Chi-squared p-values optional association measures. Without , produces one-way frequency-style summaries. Multiple output formats available via output: printed ASCII table ("default"), wide long numeric data.frame ("data.frame", "long"), publication-ready tables ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel", "clipboard", "word").

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
code: table_categorical(   data,   select,   by = NULL,   labels = NULL,   levels_keep = NULL,   include_total = TRUE,   drop_na = TRUE,   weights = NULL,   rescale = FALSE,   correct = FALSE,   simulate_p = FALSE,   simulate_B = 2000,   percent_digits = 1,   p_digits = 3,   v_digits = 2,   assoc_measure = "auto",   assoc_ci = FALSE,   decimal_mark = ".",   align = c("decimal", "center", "right"),   output = c("default", "data.frame", "long", "tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel",     "clipboard", "word"),   indent_text = "  ",   indent_text_excel_clipboard = strrep(" ", 6),   add_multilevel_header = TRUE,   blank_na_wide = FALSE,   excel_path = NULL,   excel_sheet = "Categorical",   clipboard_delim = "\t",   word_path = NULL )

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: data data frame. select Columns include row variables. Supports tidyselect syntax character vectors column names. Optional grouping column used columns/groups. Accepts unquoted column name single character column name. labels Optional display labels variables. Two forms accepted (matching table_continuous() table_continuous_lm()): named character vector whose names match column names data (e.g. c(bmi = "Body mass index")); listed columns relabelled, others fall back attribute-based labels column name. Recommended form. positional character vector length select, order. Backward-compatible spicy < 0.11.0 API. NULL (default), column names used -. variable label attribute present (e.g. haven), picked – pass labels = c(...) explicitly. (continuous companions auto-detect attribute labels; categorical function conservative indented row labels expect predictable text.) levels_keep Optional character vector levels keep/order row modalities. NULL, observed levels kept. include_total Logical. TRUE (default), includes Total group available. drop_na Logical. TRUE (default), removes rows NA row/group variable cross-tabulation. FALSE, missing values displayed dedicated "(Missing)" level. weights Optional weights. Either NULL (default), numeric vector length nrow(data), single column data supplied unquoted name character string. rescale Logical. FALSE (default), weights used -. TRUE, rescales weights total weighted N matches raw N. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). correct Logical. FALSE (default), continuity correction applied. TRUE, applies Yates correction 2x2 chi-squared contexts. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). simulate_p Logical. FALSE (default), uses asymptotic p-values. TRUE, uses Monte Carlo simulation. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). simulate_B Integer. Number Monte Carlo replicates simulate_p = TRUE. Defaults 2000. percent_digits Number digits percentages report outputs. Defaults 1. p_digits Integer >= 1. Number decimal places used render p-values p column (default: 3, APA Publication Manual standard). displayed precision small-p threshold derive argument: p_digits = 3 prints .045 <.001; p_digits = 4 prints .0451 <.0001. Leading zeros always stripped, following APA convention. v_digits Number digits association measure. Defaults 2. assoc_measure association measure report alongside chi-squared p-value. Accepts four input shapes: "none" – drop column entirely. "auto" (default) – pick measure per row variable based variable type: 2x2 table (binary row variable vs. binary ) uses phi, pair ordered factors uses tau_b, every case uses cramer_v. single string c("cramer_v", "phi", "gamma", "tau_b", "tau_c", "somers_d", "lambda") – applied uniformly every row variable. character vector one entry per row variable. named (c(smoking = "phi", health = "tau_b"), recommended; unnamed variables fall back "auto") unnamed positional (c("phi", "tau_b", "auto"), paired select) accepted. Named robust reordering select. single measure used every row, column header measure's name (e.g. "Cramer's V"). multiple measures used (typically "auto" heterogeneous select), header collapses "Effect size" APA-style Note. line appended documenting measure used variable. phi requires 2x2 table; explicitly requested non-2x2 variable, error raised user can choose another measure fall back "auto". assoc_ci Passed cross_tab(). TRUE, includes confidence interval association measure. wide raw outputs ("data.frame", "excel", "clipboard"), two extra columns CI lower / CI upper added; long raw output ("long") bounds appear ci_lower / ci_upper. rendered formats ("gt", "tinytable", "flextable", "word"), CI shown inline (e.g., .14 [.08, .19]). Defaults FALSE. decimal_mark Decimal separator ("." ","). Defaults ".". align Horizontal alignment numeric columns printed ASCII table tinytable, gt, flextable, word, clipboard outputs. first column (Variable) always left-aligned. One : "decimal" (default): align numeric columns decimal mark, standard scientific-publication convention used SPSS, SAS, LaTeX siunitx. Numeric cells pre-padded figure-spaces (U+2007, digit-width) every string column width decimal mark internal position; centring uniform-width strings stacks decimal points vertically. pad--centre strategy applied every engine (gt, tinytable, flextable, word, clipboard, ASCII print) homogeneous rendering, matching table_regression() table_continuous_lm(). "center": center-align numeric columns. "right": right-align numeric columns. excel output uses engine's default alignment case: cell-string padding align decimals proportional fonts, Excel's native right-alignment combined per-column numfmt already produces dot-aligned columns. default semantics table_continuous() / table_continuous_lm(). output Output format. One : "default" (printed ASCII table, returned invisibly) "data.frame" (wide numeric data.frame) "long" (long numeric data.frame) "tinytable" (requires tinytable) "gt" (requires gt) "flextable" (requires flextable) "excel" (requires openxlsx2) "clipboard" (requires clipr) "word" (requires flextable officer) indent_text Prefix used modality labels report table building. Defaults "  " (two spaces). indent_text_excel_clipboard Stronger indentation used Excel clipboard exports. Defaults six non-breaking spaces. add_multilevel_header Logical. TRUE (default), merges top headers Excel export. blank_na_wide Logical. FALSE (default), NA values kept -wide raw output. TRUE, replaces empty strings. excel_path Path output = "excel". Defaults NULL. excel_sheet Sheet name Excel export. Defaults "Categorical". clipboard_delim Delimiter clipboard text export. Defaults "\t". word_path Path output = "word" optional save path output = "flextable". Defaults NULL.

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: Depends output: "default": prints styled ASCII table returns underlying data.frame invisibly (S3 class "spicy_categorical_table"). "data.frame": wide data.frame one row per variable–level combination. used, columns Variable, Level, one pair n / \% columns per group level (plus Total include_total = TRUE), followed Chi2, df, p, association measure column. = NULL, columns Variable, Level, n, \%. "long": long data.frame columns variable, level, group, n, percent (chi2, df, p, association measure columns used). "tinytable": tinytable object. "gt": gt_tbl object. "flextable": flextable object. "excel" / "word": writes disk returns file path invisibly. "clipboard": copies table returns display data.frame invisibly.

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: used, selected variable cross-tabulated grouping variable cross_tab() omnibus chi-squared p-value reported p column. See @param correct / simulate_p switch Yates' continuity correction Monte Carlo p-values, @param assoc_measure per-row dispatch table used "auto" (2x2 -> Phi, ordered -> Kendall's Tau-b, otherwise Cramer's V). Without , table reports marginal frequency distribution variable inferential statistics. model-based comparisons (cluster-robust SE, weighted contrasts, fitted means) continuous outcomes, see table_continuous_lm(). descriptive (empirical) comparisons continuous outcomes, see table_continuous().

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: Decimal alignment, p-value formatting, required suggested packages per output engine documented @param align, @param p_digits, @param output respectively.

Per-variable association measure ----------------------------------  # Default (`assoc_measure = "auto"`): one measure per row variable based on # the variable type (2x2 -> Phi, both ordered factors -> Kendall's Tau-b, # otherwise Cramer's V). When the chosen measures differ across rows, the # column header collapses to `"Effect size"` and an APA-style `Note.` line # documents which measure was used for which variable. table_categorical(   sochealth,   select = c(smoking, education),   by = sex ) #> Categorical table by sex #>  #>  Variable          │ Female n  Female %  Male n  Male %  Total n  Total %   p    #> ───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  smoking           │                                                       .713  #>    No              │   475       78.4     451     79.3     926     78.8          #>    Yes             │   131       21.6     118     20.7     249     21.2          #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │                                                       .344  #>    Lower secondary │   134       21.6     127     21.9     261     21.8          #>    Upper secondary │   290       46.8     249     42.9     539     44.9          #>    Tertiary        │   196       31.6     204     35.2     400     33.3          #>  #>  Variable          │ Effect size  #> ───────────────────┼───────────── #>  smoking           │     .01      #>    No              │              #>    Yes             │              #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │     .04      #>    Lower secondary │              #>    Upper secondary │              #>    Tertiary        │              #>  #> Note. Phi: smoking; Cramer's V: education.  # Force a uniform measure across all row variables. table_categorical(   sochealth,   select = c(smoking, education),   by = sex,   assoc_measure = "cramer_v" ) #> Categorical table by sex #>  #>  Variable          │ Female n  Female %  Male n  Male %  Total n  Total %   p    #> ───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  smoking           │                                                       .713  #>    No              │   475       78.4     451     79.3     926     78.8          #>    Yes             │   131       21.6     118     20.7     249     21.2          #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │                                                       .344  #>    Lower secondary │   134       21.6     127     21.9     261     21.8          #>    Upper secondary │   290       46.8     249     42.9     539     44.9          #>    Tertiary        │   196       31.6     204     35.2     400     33.3          #>  #>  Variable          │ Cramer's V  #> ───────────────────┼──────────── #>  smoking           │    .01      #>    No              │             #>    Yes             │             #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │    .04      #>    Lower secondary │             #>    Upper secondary │             #>    Tertiary        │              # Per-variable override (recommended named form). table_categorical(   sochealth,   select = c(smoking, education, self_rated_health),   by = sex,   assoc_measure = c(     smoking           = "phi",        # binary x binary     education         = "cramer_v",   # multi-category nominal     self_rated_health = "tau_b"       # ordinal x binary, Tau-b   ) ) #> Categorical table by sex #>  #>  Variable          │ Female n  Female %  Male n  Male %  Total n  Total %   p    #> ───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  smoking           │                                                       .713  #>    No              │   475       78.4     451     79.3     926     78.8          #>    Yes             │   131       21.6     118     20.7     249     21.2          #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │                                                       .344  #>    Lower secondary │   134       21.6     127     21.9     261     21.8          #>    Upper secondary │   290       46.8     249     42.9     539     44.9          #>    Tertiary        │   196       31.6     204     35.2     400     33.3          #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  self_rated_health │                                                       .849  #>    Poor            │    31        5.1      30      5.3      61      5.2          #>    Fair            │   143       23.4     123     21.6     266     22.5          #>    Good            │   282       46.2     276     48.4     558     47.3          #>    Very good       │   154       25.2     141     24.7     295     25.0          #>  #>  Variable          │ Effect size  #> ───────────────────┼───────────── #>  smoking           │     .01      #>    No              │              #>    Yes             │              #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  education         │     .04      #>    Lower secondary │              #>    Upper secondary │              #>    Tertiary        │              #> ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌┼╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌ #>  self_rated_health │     .01      #>    Poor            │              #>    Fair            │              #>    Good            │              #>    Very good       │              #>  #> Note. Phi: smoking; Cramer's V: education; Kendall's Tau-b: self_rated_health.  # --- Output formats -----------------------------------------------------  # The rendered outputs below all wrap the same call: #   table_categorical(sochealth, #                     select = c(smoking, physical_activity), #                     by = sex) # only `output` changes. Assign each result to a variable -- some # engines auto-print as a console-friendly text fallback inside # the `?` help viewer.  # Wide data.frame (one row per modality). table_categorical(   sochealth,   select = c(smoking, physical_activity),   by = sex,   output = "data.frame" ) #>            Variable Level Female n Female % Male n Male % Total n Total % #> 1           smoking    No      475     78.4    451   79.3     926    78.8 #> 2           smoking   Yes      131     21.6    118   20.7     249    21.2 #> 3 physical_activity    No      334     53.9    316   54.5     650    54.2 #> 4 physical_activity   Yes      286     46.1    264   45.5     550    45.8 #>           p         Phi #> 1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 2 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 3 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 4 0.8316763 0.006135851  # Long data.frame (one row per (modality x group)). table_categorical(   sochealth,   select = c(smoking, physical_activity),   by = sex,   output = "long" ) #>             variable level  group   n  pct      chi2 df         p         Phi #> 1            smoking    No Female 475 78.4 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 2            smoking    No   Male 451 79.3 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 3            smoking    No  Total 926 78.8 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 4            smoking   Yes Female 131 21.6 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 5            smoking   Yes   Male 118 20.7 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 6            smoking   Yes  Total 249 21.2 0.1357733  1 0.7125196 0.010749501 #> 7  physical_activity    No Female 334 53.9 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 8  physical_activity    No   Male 316 54.5 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 9  physical_activity    No  Total 650 54.2 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 10 physical_activity   Yes Female 286 46.1 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 11 physical_activity   Yes   Male 264 45.5 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851 #> 12 physical_activity   Yes  Total 550 45.8 0.0451784  1 0.8316763 0.006135851  # \donttest{ # Rendered HTML / docx objects -- best viewed inside a # Quarto / R Markdown document or a pkgdown article. if (requireNamespace("tinytable", quietly = TRUE)) {   tt <- table_categorical(     sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,     output = "tinytable"   ) } if (requireNamespace("gt", quietly = TRUE)) {   tbl <- table_categorical(     sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,     output = "gt"   ) } if (requireNamespace("flextable", quietly = TRUE)) {   ft <- table_categorical(     sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,     output = "flextable"   ) }  # Excel and Word: write to a temporary file. if (requireNamespace("openxlsx2", quietly = TRUE)) {   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   table_categorical(     sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,     output = "excel", excel_path = tmp   )   unlink(tmp) } if (   requireNamespace("flextable", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("officer", quietly = TRUE) ) {   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".docx")   table_categorical(     sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,     output = "word", word_path = tmp   )   unlink(tmp) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Clipboard: writes to the system clipboard. table_categorical(   sochealth, select = c(smoking, physical_activity), by = sex,   output = "clipboard" ) } # }

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Computes descriptive statistics (mean, SD, min, max, confidence interval mean, n) one many continuous variables selected tidyselect syntax. , produces grouped summaries reports group-comparison p-value default (Welch test; change via test). Additional inferential output opt-: test statistics (statistic) effect sizes (effect_size / effect_size_ci). Set p_value = FALSE suppress p-value column. Without , produces one-way descriptive summaries. Multiple output formats available via output: printed ASCII table ("default"), plain data.frame ("data.frame" "long" – synonyms underlying long-format data, see Details), publication-ready tables ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel", "clipboard", "word"). descriptive companion table_continuous_lm(). two functions share layout, alignment, reporting precision descriptive model-based analyses data look uniform side side. Use table_continuous_lm() need robust SE, weighted contrasts, fitted means, covariate adjustment.

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
code: table_continuous(   data,   select = tidyselect::everything(),   by = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   regex = FALSE,   test = c("welch", "student", "nonparametric"),   p_value = NULL,   statistic = FALSE,   show_n = TRUE,   effect_size = c("none", "auto", "hedges_g", "eta_sq", "r_rb", "epsilon_sq"),   effect_size_ci = FALSE,   ci = TRUE,   labels = NULL,   ci_level = 0.95,   digits = 2,   effect_size_digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   decimal_mark = ".",   align = c("decimal", "center", "right"),   output = c("default", "data.frame", "long", "tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel",     "clipboard", "word"),   excel_path = NULL,   excel_sheet = "Descriptives",   clipboard_delim = "\t",   word_path = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: data data.frame. select Columns include. regex = FALSE, use tidyselect syntax character vector column names (default: tidyselect::everything()). regex = TRUE, provide regular expression pattern (character string). Optional grouping column. Accepts unquoted column name single character column name. Coerced factor grouping; non-numeric grouping columns (factor, character, logical) supported -. exclude Columns exclude. Supports tidyselect syntax character vectors column names. regex Logical. FALSE (default), uses tidyselect helpers. TRUE, select argument treated regular expression. test Character. Statistical test use comparing groups. One "welch" (default), "student", "nonparametric". "welch": Welch t-test (2 groups) Welch one-way ANOVA (3+ groups). assume equal variances. "student": Student t-test (2 groups) classic one-way ANOVA (3+ groups). Assumes equal variances. "nonparametric": Wilcoxon rank-sum / Mann–Whitney U (2 groups) Kruskal–Wallis H (3+ groups). Used whenever supplied (since p_value defaults TRUE case) statistic = TRUE / effect_size = TRUE. Ignored used, three display toggles turned . p_value Logical NULL. TRUE used, adds p-value column test specified test. NULL (default), p-value shown automatically whenever supplied, hidden otherwise. Pass p_value = FALSE suppress column explicitly. Ignored used. statistic Logical. TRUE used, test statistic shown additional column (e.g., t(df) = ..., F(df1, df2) = ..., W = ..., H(df) = ...). p_value statistic independent; either can enabled. Defaults FALSE. Ignored used. show_n Logical. TRUE, includes unweighted n column printed ASCII table every rendered output (tinytable, gt, flextable, word, excel, clipboard). Set FALSE drop n column structurally outputs (empty placeholder, spanner). n column always present raw output = "data.frame" / "long" downstream programmatic access. Defaults TRUE. effect_size Effect-size measure include rendered outputs. One : "none" (default): effect-size column. "auto": auto-select canonical measure chosen test group count – Hedges' g (parametric, 2 groups), eta-squared (parametric, 3+ groups), rank-biserial r (nonparametric, 2 groups), epsilon-squared (nonparametric, 3+ groups). "hedges_g": Hedges' g (bias-corrected standardised mean difference, 2 groups, parametric). CI via Hedges & Olkin normal approximation. "eta_sq": Eta-squared (\(\eta^2\), parametric ANOVA-style SS_between / SS_total). CI via inversion noncentral F distribution. "r_rb": Rank-biserial r Wilcoxon / Mann-Whitney statistic (2 groups, nonparametric). CI via Fisher z-transform. "epsilon_sq": Epsilon-squared (\(\varepsilon^2\)) Kruskal-Wallis statistic (3+ groups, nonparametric). CI via percentile bootstrap (2 000 replicates). backward compatibility, effect_size = TRUE silently coerced "auto" effect_size = FALSE "none". Explicit choices validated active test number groups; incompatible request (e.g. "eta_sq" two groups, "hedges_g" test = "nonparametric") triggers actionable error. Ignored used. effect_size_ci Logical. TRUE, appends confidence interval effect size brackets (e.g., g = 0.45 [0.22, 0.68]). Implies non-"none" effect size: left default effect_size = "none", function warns promotes effect_size "auto" requested CI can shown. Defaults FALSE. ci Logical. TRUE, includes mean confidence interval columns (<level>% CI LL / <level>% CI UL) spanner printed ASCII table every rendered output (tinytable, gt, flextable, word, excel, clipboard). Set FALSE drop columns CI spanner structurally outputs (empty placeholders, border lines empty header). CI bounds always present ci_lower / ci_upper raw output = "data.frame" / "long" downstream programmatic access. Defaults TRUE. CI level taken ci_level. labels optional named character vector variable labels. Names must match column names data. NULL (default), labels auto-detected variable attributes (e.g., haven labels); none found, column name used. ci_level Confidence level mean confidence interval (default: 0.95). Must 0 1 exclusive. digits Number decimal places descriptive values test statistics (default: 2). effect_size_digits Number decimal places effect-size values formatted displays (default: 2). p_digits Integer >= 1. Number decimal places used render p-values p column (default: 3, APA Publication Manual standard). displayed precision small-p threshold derive argument: p_digits = 3 prints .045 <.001; p_digits = 4 prints .0451 <.0001; p_digits = 2 prints .05 <.01. Useful genomics / GWAS contexts small p-values, journals using coarser convention. Leading zeros always stripped, following APA convention. decimal_mark Character used decimal separator. Either "." (default) ",". align Horizontal alignment numeric columns printed ASCII table tinytable, gt, flextable, word, clipboard outputs. first column (Variable) Group (present) always left-aligned. One : "decimal" (default): align numeric columns decimal mark, standard scientific-publication convention used SPSS, SAS, LaTeX siunitx. Numeric cells pre-padded figure-spaces (U+2007, digit-width) every string column width decimal mark internal position; centring uniform-width strings stacks decimal points vertically. pad--centre strategy applied every engine (gt, tinytable, flextable, word, clipboard, ASCII print) homogeneous rendering, matching table_regression() table_continuous_lm(). "center": center-align numeric columns. "right": right-align numeric columns. excel output uses engine's default alignment case: cell-string padding align decimals proportional fonts. default semantics table_continuous_lm(). output Output format. One : "default": printed ASCII table, returned invisibly. "data.frame" / "long": plain data.frame one row per (variable x group) (one row per variable used). two names synonyms; pick whichever reads better pipeline ("long" matches table_continuous_lm()'s naming). "tinytable" (requires tinytable) "gt" (requires gt) "flextable" (requires flextable) "excel" (requires openxlsx2) "clipboard" (requires clipr) "word" (requires flextable officer) excel_path File path output = "excel". excel_sheet Sheet name output = "excel" (default: "Descriptives"). clipboard_delim Delimiter output = "clipboard" (default: "\t"). word_path File path output = "word". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages excluded non-numeric columns (default: FALSE).

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Depends output: "default": prints styled ASCII table returns underlying data.frame invisibly (S3 class "spicy_continuous_table" / "spicy_table"). object can re-coerced via .data.frame.spicy_continuous_table() piped broom::tidy() / broom::glance(). "data.frame" / "long": plain data.frame columns variable, label, group (used), mean, sd, min, max, ci_lower, ci_upper, n. used together p_value = TRUE, statistic = TRUE, effect_size != "none", additional columns appended (populated first row variable block ): test_type – test identifier (e.g., "welch_t", "welch_anova", "student_t", "anova", "wilcoxon", "kruskal"). statistic, df1, df2, p.value – test results. es_type – effect-size identifier ("hedges_g", "eta_sq", "r_rb", "epsilon_sq"), effect_size != "none". es_value, es_ci_lower, es_ci_upper – effect-size estimate confidence interval bounds. two names "data.frame" "long" synonyms (descriptive output naturally already long). Pick whichever reads better code. "tinytable": tinytable object. "gt": gt_tbl object. "flextable": flextable object. "excel" / "word": writes disk returns file path invisibly. "clipboard": copies table returns display data.frame invisibly.

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: omnibus test computed supplied least two groups remain dropping NAs, every group contributing least two observations. Choice test family driven test (see @param entry full dispatch underlying stats:: functions called). model-based contrasts (heteroskedasticity-consistent SE, cluster-robust SE, weighted contrasts, fitted means, covariate adjustment), use table_continuous_lm().

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: See @param effect_size dispatch table (canonical measure (test, n_groups) combination) validation rules applied explicit requests. Confidence intervals (enabled effect_size_ci = TRUE) use noncentral F inversion \(\eta^2\), Hedges-Olkin normal approximation g, Fisher z-transform r, percentile bootstrap (2,000 replicates) \(\varepsilon^2\). Cohen's d, Hays' \(\omega^2\), Cohen's f\(^2\) (derived fitted, possibly weighted lm()), use model-based companion table_continuous_lm().

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Decimal alignment, p-value formatting, required suggested packages per output engine documented @param align, @param p_digits, @param output respectively. Non-numeric columns silently dropped (set verbose = TRUE see columns excluded). constant column passed, SD CI shown "--" ASCII table.

\donttest{ # Rendered HTML / docx objects -- best viewed inside a # Quarto / R Markdown document or a pkgdown article. if (requireNamespace("tinytable", quietly = TRUE)) {   tt <- table_continuous(     sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,     output = "tinytable"   ) } if (requireNamespace("gt", quietly = TRUE)) {   tbl <- table_continuous(     sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,     output = "gt"   ) } if (requireNamespace("flextable", quietly = TRUE)) {   ft <- table_continuous(     sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,     output = "flextable"   ) }  # Excel and Word: write to a temporary file. if (requireNamespace("openxlsx2", quietly = TRUE)) {   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   table_continuous(     sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,     output = "excel", excel_path = tmp   )   unlink(tmp) } if (   requireNamespace("flextable", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("officer", quietly = TRUE) ) {   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".docx")   table_continuous(     sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,     output = "word", word_path = tmp   )   unlink(tmp) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Clipboard: writes to the system clipboard. table_continuous(   sochealth, select = c(bmi, wellbeing_score), by = sex,   output = "clipboard" ) } # }

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: Builds APA-style summary tables series linear models one many continuous outcomes selected tidyselect syntax. single focal predictor supplied ; selected numeric outcome fit lm(outcome ~ , ...), optionally extended additive covariates via covariates case weights via weights. Categorical produces model-based estimated marginal means level (covariate-adjusted via adjustment covariates present), plus optional single difference dichotomous predictors. Numeric produces slope confidence interval. Inference adapts via vcov: classical OLS, "HC0"-"HC5" (heteroscedasticity-consistent), "CR0"-"CR3" (cluster-robust, requires cluster), "bootstrap" / "jackknife" resampling. Effect sizes (Cohen's "d", Hedges' "g", Hays' "omega2", Cohen's "f2") reported optional noncentral t / F confidence intervals via effect_size_ci, adapt covariate adjustment (see effect_size). Multiple output formats available via output: printed ASCII table ("default"), plain wide data.frame ("data.frame"), raw long data.frame ("long"), rendered outputs ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel", "clipboard", "word").

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
code: table_continuous_lm(   data,   select = tidyselect::everything(),   by,   covariates = NULL,   adjustment = c("proportional", "balanced"),   exclude = NULL,   regex = FALSE,   weights = NULL,   vcov = c("classical", "HC0", "HC1", "HC2", "HC3", "HC4", "HC4m", "HC5", "CR0", "CR1",     "CR2", "CR3", "bootstrap", "jackknife"),   cluster = NULL,   boot_n = 1000,   contrast = c("auto", "none"),   statistic = FALSE,   p_value = TRUE,   show_n = TRUE,   show_weighted_n = FALSE,   effect_size = c("none", "f2", "d", "g", "omega2"),   effect_size_ci = FALSE,   r2 = c("r2", "adj_r2", "none"),   ci = TRUE,   labels = NULL,   ci_level = 0.95,   digits = 2,   fit_digits = 2,   effect_size_digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   decimal_mark = ".",   align = c("decimal", "center", "right"),   output = c("default", "data.frame", "long", "tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel",     "clipboard", "word"),   excel_path = NULL,   excel_sheet = "Linear models",   clipboard_delim = "\t",   word_path = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: data data.frame. select Outcome columns include. regex = FALSE, use tidyselect syntax character vector column names (default: tidyselect::everything()). regex = TRUE, provide regular expression pattern (character string). single predictor column. Accepts unquoted column name single character column name. predictor can : numeric (continuous): treated continuous regressor. table reports slope CI lm(y ~ , ...). factor ordered factor: treated categorical. Level order preserved declared; first level reference displayed contrast (R's default treatment-contrast convention). character: coerced factor factor(), orders levels alphabetically. control reference level, supply explicit factor desired level ordering (e.g. via forcats::fct_relevel() factor(..., levels = ...)). logical: coerced factor levels "FALSE", "TRUE" (order, since FALSE < TRUE). reference level "FALSE", binary contrast displays Delta (TRUE - FALSE). Rows NA excluded analytic sample outcome (NAs y weights also excluded; see Details). covariates Optional additive covariates adjust per-outcome linear model . Accepts tidyselect expression (e.g. covariates = c(age, sex), covariates = tidyselect::all_of(cov_vec), covariates = tidyselect::starts_with("control_")) literal character vector column names. covariate must numeric, integer, logical, factor, character; covariates also appear select silently auto-excluded outcome list (variable outcome adjustment), covariate equals raises error (variable predictor adjustment). non-empty, model fitted lm(y ~ + cov1 + cov2 + ...) reported estimate / SE / p-value / CI covariate-adjusted via focal coefficient. categorical , displayed emmean covariate-adjusted estimated marginal mean – see adjustment choice estimand (G-computation default vs. equal-weight averaging). omnibus test Wald F restricted focal coefficients (computed via sandwich / clubSandwich HC* / CR* mode), adding covariates contaminate omnibus statistic covariate contributions. Effect sizes adapt automatically – see effect_size. v1 supports additive covariates . Formula syntax interactions transforms (covariates = ~ age * sex, covariates = ~ (age^2)) reserved future release; passing formula raises spicy_unsupported error migration hint. Rows NA covariate dropped analytic sample outcome (complete-cases per outcome, matching existing / weights NA handling). adjustment covariate-adjusted estimated marginal means (emmean / emmean_se / emmean_ci_* columns) computed covariates non-empty. One : "proportional" (default; matches Stata margins marginaleffects::avg_predictions()): G-computation observed sample. focal level , model predicts every observation set level (covariates kept observed values), predictions averaged. Population-weighted construction – empirical joint distribution covariates reference. Best goal "predicted mean population everyone = lvl". "balanced" (matches emmeans::emmeans() default SPSS UNIANOVA EMMEANS / SAS LSMEANS conventions): synthetic grid factor-covariate level combinations x numeric covariates fixed sample mean, grid cell weighted equally. Treats design covariates balanced – "marginal mean assuming balanced design" estimand. Best goal report covariate-purified comparison independent empirical covariate distribution. methods reduce linear-contrast formula emmean = avg_row %*% beta inherit spicy variance pipeline (HC* / CR* / bootstrap / jackknife). give answer covariates, also covariates numeric / logical (factor levels expand ). two estimands diverge least one factor / character covariate non-uniform observed proportions. exclude Columns exclude select. Supports tidyselect syntax character vectors column names. regex Logical. FALSE (default), uses tidyselect helpers. TRUE, select argument treated regular expression. weights Optional case weights. Accepts: NULL (default): ordinary unweighted lm() fit. unquoted numeric column name present data. single character column name present data. numeric vector length nrow(data) evaluated calling environment. Validation: weights must finite, non-negative, contain least one positive value (otherwise function errors). Rows NA weights excluded analytic sample outcome, alongside rows NA y . supplied, weights passed lm(..., weights = ...), coefficients become weighted least-squares estimates \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, four effect sizes computed corresponding weighted sums squares (see Weights section Details). vcov Variance estimator used standard errors, confidence intervals, Wald test statistics. One : "classical" (default): ordinary OLS/WLS variance vcov(lm), assumes homoscedastic errors. "HC0": original Eicker–White heteroskedasticity-consistent sandwich estimator (White 1980), finite-sample correction. "HC1": HC0 multiplied n / (n - p) (MacKinnon White 1985). Matches Stata's , robust default. "HC2": residuals divided sqrt(1 - h_ii) (MacKinnon White 1985). "HC3": residuals divided (1 - h_ii) (MacKinnon White 1985). common default small moderate samples (Long Ervin 2000). "HC4": leverage-adaptive variant designed influential observations (Cribari-Neto 2004). "HC4m": refinement HC4 modified leverage exponent (Cribari-Neto da Silva 2011). "HC5": alternative leverage-adaptive variant designed leveraged data (Cribari-Neto, Souza Vasconcellos 2007). "CR0", "CR1", "CR2", "CR3": cluster-robust sandwich estimators non-independent observations (Liang Zeger 1986); requires cluster. "CR2" modern default (Bell McCaffrey 2002; Pustejovsky Tipton 2018), Satterthwaite degrees freedom inference; fractional df reported df2 column t(df) / F(df1, df2) test header. "CR1" corresponds Stata's , vce(cluster id) default. Cluster-robust variants dispatched clubSandwich::vcovCR() inference uses clubSandwich::coef_test() / clubSandwich::Wald_test(); install clubSandwich use . "bootstrap": nonparametric (resampling cases) cluster bootstrap variance, depending whether cluster supplied (Davison Hinkley 1997; Cameron, Gelbach Miller 2008). number replicates set boot_n. Inference asymptotic (z single contrasts, chi^2(q) global Wald test); CIs Wald-type around point estimate. "jackknife": leave-one-variance, leave-one-cluster-cluster supplied (Quenouille 1956; MacKinnon White 1985). Inference asymptotic (z / chi^2(q)). HC* variants computed via sandwich::vcovHC(). Coefficients (means, contrasts, slopes), \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, standardized effect sizes (f2, d, g, omega2) point estimates OLS/WLS fit affected vcov; standard errors, CIs, test statistic contrast change. cluster Cluster identifier cluster-aware variance estimators. Required vcov one CR* variants; optional triggers cluster bootstrap leave-one-cluster-jackknife vcov "bootstrap" / "jackknife"; forbidden (independent-observation) variants. Accepts: NULL (default): cluster structure. unquoted column name data. single character column name data. atomic vector length nrow(data) evaluated calling environment (factor, character, integer, etc.). Rows NA cluster excluded analytic sample outcome (alongside rows NA y, , weights). least two distinct non-missing cluster values required. Multi-way clustering (list / data.frame multiple cluster vectors) supported; use sandwich::vcovCL() clubSandwich::vcovCR() directly fitted model case. boot_n Integer. Number bootstrap replicates used vcov = "bootstrap". Defaults 1000. Ignored otherwise. Larger values reduce Monte-Carlo error bootstrap variance; typical values inference 500-2000. contrast Contrast display categorical predictors. One : "auto" (default): show single reference contrast Delta (level2 - level1) exactly two non-empty levels. reference level first level factor (R's default treatment-contrast convention, getOption("contrasts")[1]). change level acts reference, re-level upstream (example forcats::fct_relevel() stats::relevel()). "none": suppress contrast column categorical predictors. Level-specific means still displayed. statistic Logical. TRUE, includes test-statistic column wide rendered outputs. Defaults FALSE. p_value Logical. TRUE, includes p column wide rendered outputs. Defaults TRUE. show_n Logical. TRUE, includes unweighted n column wide rendered outputs. Defaults TRUE. show_weighted_n Logical. TRUE weights supplied, includes Weighted n column equal sum case weights analytic sample. Defaults FALSE. effect_size Character. Effect-size column include wide rendered outputs. One : "none" (default): effect-size column. "f2": Cohen's \eqn{f^2}{f^2} = \eqn{R^2}{R^2} / (1 - \eqn{R^2}{R^2}). Defined predictor type. Familiar Cohen (1988); standard input -priori power analysis. Note single-predictor model, \eqn{f^2}{f^2} monotone transform \eqn{R^2}{R^2} adds information beyond . "d": Cohen's d = beta_hat / sigma_hat, beta_hat model coefficient (displayed difference) sigma_hat residual standard deviation fitted model. Defined exactly two non-empty levels; otherwise function errors. sign matches displayed Delta (level2 - level1). "g": Hedges' g = J * d small-sample correction J = 1 - 3 / (4 * df_resid - 1). domain "d". "omega2": Hays' omega-squared, bias-corrected estimator population variance explained, less optimistic \eqn{R^2}{R^2} small samples. Defined predictor type truncated 0. weights supplied, "d", "g", "omega2" derived weighted least-squares fit (using weighted sums squares model's weighted residual standard deviation), keeping consistent weighted contrast CI shown table. effect sizes point estimates derived OLS/WLS fit affected vcov. covariate adjustment (covariates non-empty): "f2" "omega2" become partial \(f^2\) / partial \(\omega^2\), derived partial F via stats::drop1() – correctly-defined effect size model adjusted. numeric , partial \(f^2\) equals squared partial correlation outcome, divided (1 - r^2_partial). "d" "g" raise spicy_unsupported error: Cohen's d Hedges' g canonical extension adjusted models (pooled SD undefined adjustment). Use "f2" "omega2" instead – generalise via partial F. effect_size_ci Logical. TRUE effect_size != "none", adds confidence interval effect size derived inversion appropriate noncentral distribution (noncentral t "d" / "g"; noncentral F "omega2" / "f2"). CI level taken ci_level. long output (output = "long"), bounds always present es_ci_lower / es_ci_upper (numeric). wide raw output (output = "data.frame"), bounds appear numeric columns effect_size_ci_lower / effect_size_ci_upper. printed ASCII table rendered outputs ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "word", "excel", "clipboard"), effect-size column shows value followed CI brackets (e.g. 0.18 [0.07, 0.30]). Defaults FALSE. effect_size = "none", argument ignored warning. r2 Character. Fit statistic include wide rendered outputs. One : "r2" (default): model \eqn{R^2}{R^2} (summary(lm)$r.squared). "adj_r2": adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, penalising df_effect relative residual degrees freedom. "none": omit fit-statistic column. weights supplied, \eqn{R^2}{R^2} adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2} weighted least-squares versions reported summary(lm(..., weights = ...)). ci Logical. TRUE, includes contrast confidence-interval columns wide rendered outputs single contrast shown. Defaults TRUE. labels optional named character vector outcome labels. Names must match column names data. NULL (default), labels auto-detected variable attributes; none found, column name used. ci_level Confidence level coefficient model-based mean intervals (default: 0.95). Must 0 1 exclusive. digits Number decimal places descriptive values, regression coefficients, test statistics (default: 2). fit_digits Number decimal places model-fit columns (\eqn{R^2}{R^2} adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}) wide rendered outputs (default: 2). effect_size_digits Number decimal places effect-size column (f2, d, g, omega2) wide rendered outputs (default: 2). p_digits Integer >= 1. Number decimal places used render p-values p column (default: 3, APA Publication Manual standard). displayed precision small-p threshold derive argument: p_digits = 3 prints .045 <.001; p_digits = 4 prints .0451 <.0001; p_digits = 2 prints .05 <.01. Useful genomics / GWAS contexts adjusted p-values can small, journals using coarser convention. Leading zeros always stripped, following APA convention. decimal_mark Character used decimal separator. Either "." (default) ",". align Horizontal alignment numeric columns printed ASCII table tinytable, gt, flextable, word, clipboard outputs. first column (Variable) always left-aligned. One : "decimal" (default): align numeric columns decimal mark, standard scientific-publication convention used SPSS, SAS, LaTeX siunitx. Numeric cells pre-padded figure-spaces (U+2007, digit-width) every string column width decimal mark internal position; centring uniform-width strings stacks decimal points vertically. pad--centre strategy applied every engine (gt, tinytable, flextable, word, clipboard, ASCII print) homogeneous rendering, matching table_regression(). "center": center-align numeric columns. "right": right-align numeric columns. excel output uses engine's default alignment case: cell-string padding align decimals proportional fonts, writing raw numbers numeric format require separate refactor. output Output format. One : "default": printed ASCII table, returned invisibly "data.frame": plain wide data.frame "long": raw long data.frame "tinytable" (requires tinytable) "gt" (requires gt) "flextable" (requires flextable) "excel" (requires openxlsx2) "clipboard" (requires clipr) "word" (requires flextable officer) excel_path File path output = "excel". excel_sheet Sheet name output = "excel" (default: "Linear models"). clipboard_delim Delimiter output = "clipboard" (default: "\t"). word_path File path output = "word". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages ignored non-numeric selected outcomes (default: FALSE).

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: Depends output: "default": prints styled ASCII table invisibly returns underlying long data.frame class "spicy_continuous_lm_table" / "spicy_table". "data.frame": plain wide data.frame one row per outcome numeric columns means (categorical ) slope (numeric ), optional contrast CI, optional test statistic, p, fit statistic (\eqn{R^2}{R^2} adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}), effect size, optional effect_size_ci_lower / effect_size_ci_upper (effect_size_ci = TRUE), n, Weighted n. "long": raw data.frame one block per outcome 28 columns covering identification (variable, label, predictor_type, predictor_label, level, reference), fitted means CI (emmean, emmean_se, emmean_ci_lower, emmean_ci_upper), contrast slope estimates CI (estimate_type, estimate, estimate_se, estimate_ci_lower, estimate_ci_upper), inferential output (test_type, statistic, df1, df2, p.value), effect size CI (es_type, es_value, es_ci_lower, es_ci_upper), fit (r2, adj_r2), sample size (n, weighted_n). "tinytable": tinytable object. "gt": gt_tbl object. "flextable": flextable object. "excel" / "word": writes disk returns file path. "clipboard": copies wide table returns invisibly. numeric outcome columns remain applying select, exclude, regex, function emits warning returns empty data.frame() regardless output.

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: table_continuous_lm() designed article-style reporting around single focal predictor: one model per selected continuous outcome, fitted lm(outcome ~ , ...) optionally extended case weights additive covariates (lm(outcome ~ + cov1 + ...)). categorical , reported means model-based fitted means (covariate-adjusted estimated marginal means; see adjustment) level, contrasts come fitted linear model. unweighted lm(y ~ factor) classical variance covariates, fitted means coincide numerically empirical subgroup means; model-based qualifier matters () weights means become weighted least-squares estimates, (b) CIs derive model vcov (classical, HC*, CR*, bootstrap jackknife), (c) covariates become adjusted marginal means, (d) tests, p-values effect sizes come fitted model, keeping table internally consistent. Compared table_continuous(), function model-based companion: choose want heteroskedasticity-consistent standard errors (vcov = "HC*"), model fit statistics, case weights via lm(..., weights = ...). function exists report fitted model, inferential output default: p_value = TRUE r2 = "r2" defaults; set p_value = FALSE r2 = "none" suppress .

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: Effect size selected explicitly via effect_size (defaults "none"). variants derived fitted model displayed coefficients, \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, CIs, effect size stays internally consistent rest table. "f2": Cohen's \eqn{f^2}{f^2} = \eqn{R^2}{R^2} / (1 - \eqn{R^2}{R^2}) (Cohen 1988). Defined predictor type. single-predictor model, \eqn{f^2}{f^2} monotone transform \eqn{R^2}{R^2} adds information beyond ; primary use priori power analysis (e.g. G*Power). "d", "g": standardized mean difference (Cohen's d Hedges' g), defined exactly two non-empty levels. d = beta_hat / sigma_hat sigma_hat = summary(fit)$sigma (pooled within-group SD unweighted two-group case); g = J * d J = 1 - 3 / (4 * df_resid - 1) (Hedges Olkin 1985). sign matches displayed Delta (level2 - level1). published reports two-group comparisons, g convention recommended Hedges Olkin (1985). "omega2": Hays' \(\omega^2\), computed weighted sums squares (SS_effect - df_effect * MSE) / (SS_total + MSE) truncated 0 small null effects (Hays 1963; Olejnik Algina 2003). Less biased \(\eta^2\) (equals R^2 single-predictor design) recommended reporting variance explained ANOVA-style designs (Olejnik Algina 2003). four effect sizes point estimates derived OLS/WLS fit invariant vcov: choosing HC* changes SE, CI, test statistic contrast standardized magnitude . covariate adjustment (covariates non-empty), "f2" "omega2" become partial \(f^2\) / partial \(\omega^2\) , derived partial F via stats::drop1() restricted focal term. "d" "g" raise spicy_unsupported error: pooled standard deviation canonical extension adjustment, Cohen's d Hedges' g undefined adjusted models. See effect_size full dispatch. Confidence intervals effect size available via effect_size_ci = TRUE use modern noncentral-distribution inversion approach, consensus standard commercial statistical software (Stata esize / estat esize, SAS PROC TTEST PROC GLM EFFECTSIZE 14.2+) mainstream R packages (effectsize, MOTE, TOSTER, effsize): "d", "g": noncentral t inversion (Steiger Fouladi 1997; Goulet-Pelletier Cousineau 2018). Empirical coverage nominal across sample sizes (Cousineau Goulet-Pelletier 2021), unlike older Hedges-Olkin normal approximation biased small samples. Hedges' g bounds inherit J small-sample correction. "omega2", "f2": noncentral F inversion (Steiger 2004). Bounds converted noncentrality parameter using omega^2 = ncp / (ncp + N) \eqn{f^2}{f^2} = ncp / N respectively, N = df1 + df2 + 1 (total sample size). weighted case, CI uses raw (unweighted) group counts df.residual(fit) = n - p, consistent WLS reporting convention (DuMouchel Duncan 1983). propensity-score balance assessment complex-survey designs, dedicated packages (cobalt::bal.tab() Austin Stuart 2015 formulation; survey design-based effect sizes) appropriate.

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: vcov one HC* variants, standard errors, CIs, Wald test statistics use heteroskedasticity-consistent sandwich estimator computed via sandwich::vcovHC() (Zeileis 2004), canonical R implementation. brief guide: "HC0" original White (1980) form; "HC1" adds n / (n - p) correction (MacKinnon White 1985), Stata's , robust default. "HC2" "HC3" use leverage-based residual rescalings (MacKinnon White 1985); "HC3" sandwich::vcovHC() default small moderate samples (Long Ervin 2000). "HC4" adapts leverage exponent influential observations (Cribari-Neto 2004); "HC4m" modified-exponent refinement (Cribari-Neto da Silva 2011); "HC5" alternative leverage-adaptive variant (Cribari-Neto, Souza Vasconcellos 2007). observations independent (repeated measurements per individual, students nested classes, patients hospitals, country-year panels), classical HC* standard errors biased downward. Use CR* variants together cluster = id_var get cluster-robust inference (Liang Zeger 1986). implementation dispatches clubSandwich::vcovCR() variance clubSandwich::coef_test() (single-coefficient, Satterthwaite t) clubSandwich::Wald_test() (multi-coefficient Hotelling-T-squared Satterthwaite df, "HTZ") inference. "CR2" (Bell McCaffrey 2002; Pustejovsky Tipton 2018) modern recommended default; generally produces fractional Satterthwaite degrees freedom df2, displayed t(df) / F(df1, df2) header renders one decimal. "CR1" matches Stata's , vce(cluster id). Effect sizes remain invariant vcov (including CR*); SE, CI, test statistic, df2 contrast change. Two resampling-based estimators also available without adding dependency: vcov = "bootstrap" (nonparametric resampling-cases bootstrap; Davison Hinkley 1997) vcov = "jackknife" (leave-one-delete-1; Quenouille 1956; MacKinnon White 1985). Supplying cluster switches cluster-aware variants (cluster bootstrap, Cameron, Gelbach Miller 2008; leave-one-cluster-jackknife). number bootstrap replicates controlled boot_n (default 1000); replicates fail fit rank-deficient resamples dropped, explicit warning half fail fallback classical OLS variance 10 valid replicates. Inference estimators asymptotic (z single-coefficient contrasts, chi^2(q) multi-coefficient global Wald test k > 2 categorical predictors), reflected displayed test header. Use bootstrap residual distribution non-standard sample small; use jackknife closed-form, deterministic alternative. \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, effect sizes remain ordinary least-squares (weighted least-squares) statistics regardless vcov.

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: weights supplied, table_continuous_lm() fits weighted linear models via lm(..., weights = ...). Means become weighted least-squares estimates contrasts slopes weighted. fit statistics \eqn{R^2}{R^2} adjusted \eqn{R^2}{R^2}, well Hays' omega^2 Cohen's \eqn{f^2}{f^2}, use corresponding weighted sums squares WLS fit. Cohen's d Hedges' g use WLS coefficient model's weighted residual standard deviation (summary(fit)$sigma), standard convention case-weighted regression-style reporting (DuMouchel Duncan 1983); noncentral t CI d / g uses raw (unweighted) group counts residual degrees freedom WLS fit (n - p). case-weighted workflow appropriate weighted article tables, substitute full complex-survey design (see e.g. survey package), propensity-score balance assessment Austin Stuart (2015) convention (see e.g. cobalt::bal.tab()). n column always reports unweighted analytic sample size outcome. show_weighted_n = TRUE, additional Weighted n column reports sum case weights analytic sample.

title: Continuous-outcome linear-model table — table_continuous_lm
text: dichotomous categorical predictors, wide outputs report fitted means reference-level order label contrast column explicitly Delta (level2 - level1). categorical predictors two levels, single contrast contrast CI shown wide outputs; instead, table reports level-specific means plus overall F test statistic = TRUE (F(df1, df2) degrees freedom constant across outcomes). covariates non-empty, printed ASCII table appends APA-style footer naming covariates chosen estimand, e.g. Note. Adjusted age, education (proportional). Optional output engines require corresponding suggested packages: tinytable output = "tinytable" gt output = "gt" flextable output = "flextable" flextable + officer output = "word" openxlsx2 output = "excel" clipr output = "clipboard"
